beat.title: Hall of Pillars
beat.boundaryReason: The session opens with table preamble and recap, then settles into a single exploration scene in the damaged hall as the party studies the black pillars, inspects the keystone, debates the lower hatches, and ends when Eolo tries to toss the stone away and the group snaps into initiative order.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets both drStart and drEnd to 1730-01-29
the recap resumes the party exactly where the previous session ended, immediately after the orc fight in the hall
nothing before the initiative call indicates a rest, sunset, or any advance beyond the same afternoon

beat.title: Stone Returned to Eidrikas
beat.boundaryReason: Once initiative starts, the scene focuses on suppressing the stone's charm, containing it safely, consulting Eidrikas about the machine and Melusa, and deciding on a short rest and return trip, which forms a coherent post-retrieval resolution before the explicit rest begins.
dateEvidence: this beat begins immediately after the hall exploration scene breaks into initiative to handle the charm effect
the party remains on the same side of the portal through the entire discussion with Eidrikas and does not yet rest
the DM only places dusk and stars after the short rest decision, so this beat remains in the late afternoon of 1730-01-29

beat.title: Night Return to Melusa
beat.boundaryReason: The beat begins with the short rest that carries the party into starlight, then follows their passage back through the portal, the wisp swarm attack on arrival, and their first survey of the darkened, abandoned-feeling edge of Melusa before they commit to a rescue plan.
dateEvidence: the DM says that by the time the short rest ends 'you can see stars,' which is the clearest time-of-day cue in the session
the portal crossing and wisp encounter happen immediately after that rest with no suggestion of sleep or a new day
the beat ends before any further time skip, so the date stays 1730-01-29 while the time window shifts to night

beat.title: Refugees Gathered
beat.boundaryReason: After deciding not to visit the palace, the party looks for willing escapees, finds Zahara's group, learns what happened after the palace fell dark, and works out the first rescue concept until Finnan turns from planning to actively rousing the town.
dateEvidence: this beat starts directly after the party finishes surveying the village edge and chooses a concrete objective: find anyone willing to leave
all events are one continuous sequence of movement and conversation inside Melusa with no rest or implied downtime
the prior beat already established that it is night after the short rest, and nothing here advances the calendar day

beat.title: The Town Crier's Plea
beat.boundaryReason: Finnan's public call turns the rescue effort into a town-wide social scene: villagers emerge, an older skeptic challenges him, the party weighs whether to involve the fae lord, and they finally settle on rescuing the smaller willing group before cutting to round-by-round execution.
dateEvidence: the beat begins when Finnan stops planning and starts broadcasting through the village
the transcript tracks a continuous attempt to persuade and sort the crowd rather than any travel or rest

beat.title: Evacuation Through the Wisps
beat.boundaryReason: Once the party starts running the rescue in rounds, the session shifts into a sustained tactical evacuation beat as Justas and Ekko draw the wisps, Eolo receives refugees on the far side, and the group gets dozens of people through before Eidrikas reinstalls the keystone and the portal begins to fail.
dateEvidence: this beat starts exactly where the transcript changes from planning to explicit round-by-round action
the rescue and wisp distraction play out as one continuous tactical scene with no pause
the transcript still treats it as the same night in Melusa and at the portal platform, with no evidence of dawn or a date change

beat.title: Aftermath and Debrief
beat.boundaryReason: After the keystone is restored and the immediate rescue ends, the transcript moves into aftermath planning, refugee logistics, summary narration of the broader evacuation, lore questions about the chasm machinery, and then table-level campaign debrief and session closeout.
dateEvidence: this beat begins immediately after the portal starts being scraped off the wall and the action shifts away from the active rescue
the transcript discusses camp, bedrolls, and plans for 'the morning' but never actually advances into the next day
because the morning is only planned rather than played, the entire closing stretch remains on 1730-01-29 at night
